input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

data.content:
Yes, both approaches are correct for .NET MAUI on .NET 10.

**`ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"`** — This is the iOS platform-specific that insets your page content to avoid the notch/Dynamic Island. You need the namespace:

```xml
xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
```

Or in C#:

```csharp
On<iOS>().SetUseSafeArea(true);
```

**`IgnoreSafeArea="True"`** — This lets a specific layout extend *back into* the safe area, even when the page has `UseSafeArea` enabled. Perfect for background images that should go edge-to-edge while keeping other content inset.

```xml
<ContentPage ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True" ...>
  <Grid>
    <Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
      <Image Source="bg.png" Aspect="AspectFill" />
    </Grid>
    <!-- Other content stays safely inset -->
    <Label Text="Hello" />
  </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

Both APIs are stable and the recommended approach in .NET MAUI. Your tutorial is on the right track.
